name: AUTOPHYES
tagline: CONSUME, EXPLOIT, RETAIN AT ALL COST
description: Post-industrial horrors consumed by an elemental greed. Their sole focus: to tear up all the realms of Hell to sate their bottomless avarice.

startingLocations.name: Omphalos
startingLocations.description: The Tower. From there, HAAGENTI can see all. The Tower. Its interior bleeds with ANATHEMA, her very essence fused into its structure. The Tower. All the wealth of a thousand reaved worlds, gathered in one place. The Tower. It calls to you. It knows that you Want. Her eyes pierce heart and mind like sunlight pierces glass. The Tower. Approach. Approach and be found wanting. Approach and be damned. The Tower. The Tower. The Tower.

startingLocations.name: Mnemosyne Garden
startingLocations.description: A dissonant sight in a desolate realm, the Garden surrounding HAAGENTI's tower is a verdant sanctuary for the flora and fauna of a thousand different origins. The places these wonders come from are dead now, of course, chewed and swallowed up and turned to spear tips and wagon wheels. Their memory remains only so long as their orphans continue to entertain and stimulate AUTOPHYES's overlord.
startingLocations.purpose: Guided meditation, mindfulness exercises, and positive self-affirmation.
startingLocations.terrain: Edenic hedgerows and orchards, paved paths
startingLocations.defenses: Perimeter wall of overgrown razorsharp wireweal, fearful exotic beasts, territorial gardeners

startingLocations.name: Ouroboric Manufactoria
startingLocations.description: The heart of Hell's war machine. Clusters of artless, blunt buildings overlapping and growing out of each other like cancer cells, smokestacks piled higher and higher until their clouds of black smog choke out the sky.
startingLocations.purpose: Build, build, build, grow, build, grow, build
startingLocations.terrain: Conveyer belt tangles, enormous factory buildings, piles of canceled orders
startingLocations.defenses: Pit boss Bulwarks, confusing layout, hungry machinery

startingLocations.name: River of Nepenthe
startingLocations.description: The profane waters of the Nepenthe stem from the outflow of thousands of chemical processes undergone in Hell's foundries, factories, and abattoirs. Flowing freely along cuts in the soul, the river eventually makes its way into Acherousia. A primordial Lake of Cleansing forever drowned in arsenic and benzine. It's also a logistics route, smoke-belching paddlewheelers carrying supplies and soldiers to the front lines.
startingLocations.purpose: Logistical, boundary, and industrial waste route
startingLocations.terrain: Skeleton-lined river bank, floating islands of indescribable effluent, the river itself

startingLocations.name: Drained Grail
startingLocations.description: An enormous open pit mine that once fed the factories further into the interior. Now nothing comes out of the Grail but Yearnlings. These insect-like creatures dig a network of unstable, ever shifting tunnels that allow them to gather intelligence, as well as burst out of the ground for surprise strikes on vulnerable areas.
startingLocations.terrain: Bottomless pit, old mining equipment, labyrinthine tunnel network
startingLocations.defenses: A (seemingly) infinite amount of Yearnlings, difficult to scout tunnels

startingLocations.name: March of Marasmos
startingLocations.description: Without new worlds to conquer, AUTOPHYES's insatiable engines are often forced to cannibalize itself, leaving nothing behind but sand and hundreds of hundreds of open pits. This is a desert more akin to Luna's surface than anything on Earth. There are few places to seek refuge, and HAAGENTI's forces know them all.
startingLocations.purpose: Defensive, acting as a vast and hostile buffer zone
startingLocations.terrain: Impossibly vast wasteland, abandoned mining shafts and hidden sinkholes
startingLocations.defenses: A complete absence of hospitability, far-ranging Extractor patrols, shambling mutants looking for some quiet place to die.

uniqueLocations.name: The Nihilith
uniqueLocations.description: A smaller copy of HAAGENTI's own headquarters, this monolithic structure is nevertheless an imposing, disconcerting sight. In a land of constant attrition and reuse, its edifice remains impregnable.
uniqueLocations.purpose: Localized Command and Control. Unlocks the Bulwark minion once constructed.
uniqueLocations.terrain: Inscrutable sheer walls of polished obsidian which drain the life out of anyone who dares touch it without permission, shoulder-high barrier walls made of same arranged in geometric patterns
uniqueLocations.defenses: Bulwarks on constant patrol, occasional processions of worshippers and supplicants bringing meagre tributes and will hastily snap into violent rages if the Nihilith is attacked

uniqueLocations.name: Boneyard
uniqueLocations.description: A seemingly endless expanse of corpses both mechanical and biological in various stages of decay. Extractors compete with nomadic scavengers and shambling horrors made of neglect for each potentially useful component or organ. Nothing is left to rest forever.
uniqueLocations.purpose: Salvage and repurposing the ancient dead
uniqueLocations.terrain: Disorienting labyrinth of the dead and dying forming walls that grow taller if you try to climb them, pools of rendering flesh and oil, piles of rusting components and weathered bones

uniqueLocations.name: Crooked Obelisk
uniqueLocations.description: Normally placed on a chokepoint or high ground, this enormous obsidian obelisk resembled a crooked, taloned finger. After a substantial charging timer, it fires a coherent beam of anti-light that can turn anything and anyone to purest void. Unless destroyed, no one may pass.
uniqueLocations.terrain: Tight, cramped corridors concealing pipes, compressors, and other machinery, an enormous onyx crystal called an ANATHEMATIC Focusing Iris installed at the top
uniqueLocations.defenses: Nuclear semiotic hellscape of smaller obsidian structures, high ground or otherwise defensible position, a defensive garrison, the Obelisk's ire

uniqueLocations.name: Abyssal Exsanguinator
uniqueLocations.description: An enormous pumpjack whose pipes stab deep into Hell's surface. A dark crimson fluid bubbles and pulses to the surface that scholars and madmen posit to be the damned souls of eons past, compressed by millions of years and the weight of billions of tons of pressure into a fluid.
uniqueLocations.purpose: Draw the hellblood from fresh wounds
uniqueLocations.terrain: Clusters of machinery, long rows of pipes, broken and cracked earth
uniqueLocations.defenses: Work crews, dangerous machinery, high pressure vessels filled with Hellblood ready to pop at a single errant bullet

uniqueLocations.name: Vault of Plenty
uniqueLocations.description:
One of the main reserves for the accumulated wealth of the faction, guarded above all else. Worshipped (from the outside) by those under the AUTOPHYES domain. Somewhere between the Parthenon, a Bank Vault, and that place where rich people keep their blackmail.

GM gains 2 Ichor every turn instead of 1. This building can only be built if one of the players has mentioned some armour, weapon, or other feature that they really want in the game. It is in this vault. Tell them, and only them, that it is in this vault. Also tell them that if they let anyone else know, it is no longer in this vault. Some resources are in the vault instead. Enough to satiate, but not enough to prevent people going "that's it?". Yes. That's it.
uniqueLocations.purpose: GM gains 2 Ichor every turn instead of 1.
uniqueLocations.terrain: Lobbies of marble and gold concealing defenses, crystal tesseract chandeliers and other ostentatious wealth beyond measuring sat out to gawk at, enormous vaults the size of houses. Deeper in, cracks in the walls have let black ooze seep in.
uniqueLocations.defenses: This building is defended as if it is a literal death trap. Which it is. Nesting, interconnected defense in depth manned with every possible demonic minion. If all the forces currently deployed to guard this Vault were to assault DIS, the city would fall.

stratcomActions.name: Scratch Them Out
stratcomActions.flavor: The Crooked Obelisk fires a focused and condensed beam of hungry nothing that grants oblivion to all those unlucky enough to be in its path. Death is not the end. The void is.
stratcomActions.description: During your next TACCOM Engagement, at the beginning of Turn 1 draw a straight line either horizontally or vertically across the entirety of the play space. At the start of the next turn (Turn 2), do 15 damage to anything inside that line. The Obelisk must spend the next turn charging, but can be used again starting on Turn 4, repeating the cycle of Aim Line, Fire, Charge until the battle is over.

stratcomActions.name: Wireweal Outbreak
stratcomActions.flavor: Though primitive, the wireweals demonstrate a kind of intelligence not unlike a pack animal. Through a mix of droning sirens and Pavlovian behavioral conditioning, even the hylic manifestation of the land's hollow soul can be used as a weapon.
stratcomActions.description: Temporarily enclose a location in clusters of Rust Palisade Fortifications. Attacking this Location any time this turn or the next will cause 5 Blob:5 clusters of Wireweal to be added to the defenses, placed in areas either protecting AUTOPHYES interests or in ways to inconvenience the players. On turn 2 and every 2nd turn after that, add another Blob:5 cluster.

upgrades.name: Depleted Uranium Claws
upgrades.flavor: Some of the intruders into the AUTOPHYES domain are tougher to pull apart. As always, the answer to their problem lies within industrial waste.
upgrades.effect: +1 Damage increase in Extractor's base attacks, enemies analyzed with The Right Tool now take 8 damage per attack.

upgrades.name: Burrowing Frenzy
upgrades.flavor: Targeting the rapacious flailing of the Yearnling swarms has been a constant struggle, but some progress has been made using hypothalamic stimulator implants to drive them into temporary magic digging for tactical purposes.
upgrades.effect: Yearnlings can now burrow beneath the ground, protecting them from damage while allowing them to move at half their normal Speed. This can be used defensively to protect them from incoming AOE damage, to burrow underneath large obstacles, or to create minefields of swarming death to spring on unsuspecting interlopers.

upgrades.name: Cerebrospinal Propellants
upgrades.flavor: Dogs are a feature around junk yards throughout existence. AUTOPHYES believes that if they were going to loiter, then they better start putting in shifts.

upgrades.name: Skinweaver Looms
upgrades.flavor: New horrors are made every day in Hell's sweatshops. Any advances in automation are put to use, but are nevertheless supplemented by an increase in workers performing extra steps to offset the productivity increase. Suffering, you see, is a part of the recipe.

upgrades.name: Hellblood Infusion Regimen
upgrades.flavor: The fluid extracted by the pumps has several interesting properties. In particular, its desire to escape, at all costs. This can give demonic forces an increase in battlefield initiative, with the minor downside that the hellblood inevitably explodes out of their body before long.
upgrades.effect: Once per TACCOM Engagement, temporarily increase the base Speed of a unit by 3 for 3 turns. At the start of the fourth turn, this unit loses half their starting health.

upgrades.name: Thanatological Punch Cards
upgrades.flavor: Your absence from the battlefield has been noted by your supervisor. Due to your contract's clause on unsanctioned cessation of work, your immortal soul is currently withheld. Further attempts to expire without permission will result in docked wages.
upgrades.effect: AUTOPHYES units with the Minion tag that die within a 3x3 square box of Bulwark minions enter a null state for a turn, then are brought back to life at full health.

upgrades.name: Hematic Combustion
upgrades.flavor: Hellblood's motive force is finally harnessed to do some evil. Namely, give life to a previously abandoned drilling project so terrible in its drive and execution that some in HAAGENTI's inner circle were circumspect about its deployment. If you squint, you can still see some pieces of them in the treads.
upgrades.effect: Unlock deployment of The Engine miniboss.

upgrades.name: ANATHEMATIC Condensers
upgrades.flavor: The hungry goo that sloughs off HAAGENTI at regular intervals can be harnessed and compressed into creating a new, somehow even more disturbing material.
upgrades.effect: Unlocks construction of the Crooked Obelisk and the Nihilith locations.

upgrades.name: Apotheotic Avarice
upgrades.flavor: A cult of desire and envy lurks in the heart of every weary soul damned to walk the wastes of Hell. A religion that offers some measure of hope, some flicker of a future. It tells them that ascension is possible but only through pure, unalloyed greed. Only in realizing you are nothing but your wants can you truly become what you are.
upgrades.effect: Place the Vault of Plenty location.

upgrades.name: Carcinogenic Defensive Upgrade
upgrades.flavor: Controlling the spread of the tangled barricades and concertina wire that burst forth unbidden from the soil had been much of the focus of HAAGENTI's interest in the wireweal phenomena. Not to mitigate its spread, but to direct its insensate ire.
upgrades.effect: Unlock the Wireweal Outbreak STRATCOM Action.
